user.title: Add PIK Outlet
user.description: Enter the Bluetooth address of your PIK 6-Switch Outlet, or select a discovered device.

bluetooth_confirm.title: Confirm PIK Outlet
bluetooth_confirm.description: Do you want to set up **{name}**?

abort.no_devices_found: No PIK Outlet devices found nearby.
abort.cannot_connect: Could not connect to the device. Make sure it is powered on and in range.

error.cannot_connect: Could not connect to the device.

set_timer_enable.name: Set Timer Enable
set_timer_enable.description: Enable or disable timer schedules for each socket.

flags.name: Timer Flags
flags.description: 6-character string of '0' and '1' for sockets 1-6. Example: '110101' enables timers on sockets 1, 2, 4, 6.

set_timer_profile.name: Set Timer Profile
set_timer_profile.description: Configure a timer schedule profile for a socket.

profile.name: Profile Slot
profile.description: Profile slot number (1-6). Each socket supports up to 6 independent schedules.

days.name: Days Bitmask
days.description: Weekday bitmask (0-127). bit0=Sun, bit1=Mon, bit2=Tue, bit3=Wed, bit4=Thu, bit5=Fri, bit6=Sat. Example: 62 = Mon-Sat.

send_raw_command.name: Send Raw Command
send_raw_command.description: Send a raw PIK-CMD command to the device (for debugging and advanced use).

command.name: Command
command.description: Command string without 'PIK-CMD:' prefix. Example: 'STATUS' or 'CUSTOM:AT+VER'.

sync_time.name: Sync Time
sync_time.description: Synchronize the device RTC clock to Home Assistant's current time.
